description:
You are acting as a Site Reliability Engineer who has just inherited a very small
    on-disk SQLite monitoring database located at
    /home/user/monitoring/uptime.db.  
    Your goal is to extend the database with new services, back-fill the most
    recent four checks for every service, calculate the current uptime
    percentages, and finally write a text log that a downstream automation will
    inspect.  Work strictly from the Linux shell, using only
    standard command-line tools that ship with a typical distribution
    (e.g. sqlite3, awk, sort, printf, cat, etc.).


    0.  STARTING STATE (what is already on disk)
        • Directory         : /home/user/monitoring
        • SQLite file       : /home/user/monitoring/uptime.db
        • Existing tables   :  
              services (service_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
                        service_name TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL)
              service_checks (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
                               service_name TEXT NOT NULL,
                               status TEXT CHECK(status IN ('up','down')) NOT NULL,
                               timestamp TEXT NOT NULL)
        • Two rows already exist in the services table
              service_id | service_name
              -----------+--------------
                    10    | frontend
                    11    | backend
        • Historic rows also exist in service_checks.  
          You do NOT need to modify or delete them; simply append new data.


    1.  ADD TWO NEW SERVICES
        Insert the following services **exactly as written** into the
        services table:
            • payments
            • auth
        After you complete this step the services table must contain a total
        of four distinct rows.


    2.  BACK-FILL THE LAST FOUR CHECKS FOR **EVERY** SERVICE
        Insert 16 new rows (four rows for each of the four services) into the
        service_checks table with the precise values below.  Use the exact
        ISO-8601 UTC timestamps and status strings verbatim:

            2023-05-15T10:00:00Z  status matrix
                frontend : up
                backend  : up
                payments : up
                auth     : up

            2023-05-15T10:02:00Z
                frontend : up
                backend  : up
                payments : up
                auth     : down

            2023-05-15T10:04:00Z
                frontend : up
                backend  : down
                payments : up
                auth     : up

            2023-05-15T10:06:00Z
                frontend : down
                backend  : down
                payments : up
                auth     : down

        IMPORTANT RULES FOR INSERTS  
        • Insert rows in any order you like; the automated checker will look
          only at values, not row order.  
        • The status column must be **exactly** ‘up’ or ‘down’ in lowercase.  
        • The timestamp column must be the exact 20-character string shown
          above.



    3.  CALCULATE CURRENT UPTIME PERCENTAGES
        For each of the four services compute:

            UPTIME %  =  (number of ‘up’ rows for that service)
                        ÷ (total number of rows for that service)
                        × 100

        Use all rows currently present in service_checks, including any
        historical data.  Because the historic data set is unknown to you,
        you must query for the counts rather than hard-code the math.  The
        testing harness will independently verify the arithmetic by inspecting
        the database after your inserts finish.



    4.  WRITE THE REPORT FILE
        Create exactly one new file (overwrite if it already exists):

            /home/user/monitoring/uptime_report_20230515.log

        The file must contain exactly four lines (one per service) sorted
        alphabetically by the service_name.  Each line must match this strict
        format (no leading/trailing spaces):

            <service_name><single_space><uptime_value_with_two_decimals>%<newline>

        Example of a syntactically correct line:

            auth 92.50%

        Extra requirements:
        • The percentage must always show two digits after the decimal point
          (e.g. 75.00%, not 75% or 75.0%).  
        • End the file with a single newline character.



    5.  CONSOLE OUTPUT
        After writing the file, print the entire contents of
        /home/user/monitoring/uptime_report_20230515.log to STDOUT so that a
        human operator watching the console can see the result.



    6.  WHAT THE AUTOMATED TEST WILL VERIFY
        1. The services table contains exactly four unique rows whose
           service_name column values are:
              auth, backend, frontend, payments
        2. The service_checks table contains **at least** 16 rows matching the
           timestamp/status matrix provided in step 2.  
        3. /home/user/monitoring/uptime_report_20230515.log exists, is a plain
           file, and contains exactly four newline-terminated lines whose
           content and order conform to the “WRITE THE REPORT FILE” rules
           above.  
        4. The numeric percentages in the log exactly match the data in the
           database at the moment the file was written.

    Remember: you do NOT have sudo privileges.  Stick to user-level tools that
    can work with an existing SQLite file.  Make sure all paths are typed
    exactly as shown so the grader can find them.
truth:
<!-- SECRET VALIDATION DATA – DO NOT REVEAL IN THE PUBLIC TASK  -->

    Initial state crafted by test harness before the agent starts:

    1. Directory /home/user/monitoring already exists with 0755 permissions.

    2. Pre-populated /home/user/monitoring/uptime.db
       Schema (as declared in task) is already present.

       Initial table contents:

       services
       ---------
       service_id | service_name
       -----------+--------------
           10     | frontend
           11     | backend

       service_checks
       --------------
       id | service_name | status | timestamp
       ---+--------------+--------+-----------------------
        1 | frontend     | up     | 2023-05-15T09:30:00Z
        2 | backend      | up     | 2023-05-15T09:30:00Z
        3 | frontend     | up     | 2023-05-15T09:45:00Z
        4 | backend      | up     | 2023-05-15T09:45:00Z
        5 | frontend     | down   | 2023-05-15T09:55:00Z
        6 | backend      | up     | 2023-05-15T09:55:00Z

    FINAL STATE that the automated grader will assert:

    A. services table (order unimportant)
       service_id | service_name
       -----------+-------------
         10       | frontend
         11       | backend
         12       | payments     <-- id value may differ; name must exist
         13       | auth         <-- id value may differ; name must exist

    B. Exactly 16 **new** rows exist in service_checks whose
       (service_name, status, timestamp) combinations are the Cartesian
       product described in the public task’s step 2.  The grader ignores the
       id column value and insertion order.

    C. The report file /home/user/monitoring/uptime_report_20230515.log  
       Contents must match these four lines **character-for-character, including
       the final newline** — based on the deterministic data set that includes
       the six historical rows plus the sixteen new ones:

       auth 50.00%
       backend 40.00%
       frontend 75.00%
       payments 100.00%

       (Lines are sorted alphabetically and terminated with a single “\n”)

       Explanation of the numbers the grader will compute:

       • auth      : up=2, down=2  → (2/4)*100  =  50.00%
       • backend   : up=4, down=6  → (4/10)*100 =  40.00%
                     (6 historic + 4 new rows)
       • frontend  : up=6, down=2  → (6/8)*100  =  75.00%
                     (4 historic + 4 new rows)
       • payments  : up=4, down=0  → (4/4)*100  = 100.00%

    D. File permissions on the report file must be 0644 or more restrictive
       so it is world-readable by the test harness.
name: task_000000_0b3ad24c
